resolvedQuestions: Q1: The automated machinery is bifurcated and surface-incomplete; skill docs, references, command docs, and context-engineering assets have NO automated data-quality gate.
Q2: Adherence/logic automations are write-time and floor-bypassing (features 1-5, half-built); the one retrieval automation (inject->refine loop, feature 6) PAYS the floor and needs prod-mode proof.
Q3: Highest-ROI set = features 1-5; rank 1 (wire the existing DQI scorer on-write + retroactively) is the single highest-ROI move.

keyFindings.label: All 33 validator-registry rules are spec-doc-scoped; none target skill/reference/command/context-engineering docs
The pre-commit hook gates code/MCP/prompt-registry integrity (5 gates) but never document data-quality or DQI
sk-doc extract_structure.py computes a deterministic type-aware DQI with per-type gates but is never automated
Skill/reference/command docs carry floor-bypassing retrieval+adherence frontmatter with zero automated quality gate
generate-context post-save-review auto-reviews trigger_phrase quality but only on spec-folder saves
memory:manage has retroactive HYGIENE ops but none refine source-doc data quality
advisor_validate / skill_graph_validate are routing-metadata validators, orthogonal to doc DQI
The injection layer is a pure consumer with no quality feedback loop; inject->refine is the missing retrieval-class automation and PAYS the floor

ruledOutDirections.direction: A corpus-wide retroactive doc-quality sweep already exists
ruledOutDirections.reason: validation.md batch snippets are illustrative, no shipped runner

ruledOutDirections.direction: memory:manage or advisor_validate IS the doc-quality automation
ruledOutDirections.reason: both operate on the index/routing axis, not the source-document data-quality axis
